task_completion.description: Eclipse IDE is open and the 'DevUtils' project is visible in the Package Explorer or Project Explorer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Eclipse IDE window is open with 'DevUtils' listed in the left-hand project navigation pane.

task_completion.description: The 'Generate Javadoc' dialog window is opened in Eclipse.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box titled 'Generate Javadoc' is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The Javadoc command path is correctly configured to the specified JDK path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Generate Javadoc' dialog, the 'Javadoc command' text field contains exactly '/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64/bin/javadoc'.

task_completion.description: The 'Protected' visibility is selected and the DevUtils packages are checked for documentation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Generate Javadoc' dialog, the 'Protected' radio button is selected under 'Visibility', and the packages (com.devutils.text, com.devutils.math, com.devutils.collection) are checked in the types list.

task_completion.description: The destination folder for the generated Javadoc is set to the correct output path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Generate Javadoc' dialog, the 'Destination' text field is set to '/home/ga/javadoc-output'.

task_completion.description: The Javadoc generation process is executed and completes successfully.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Eclipse Console view shows the Javadoc generation output (e.g., 'Loading source files...', 'Generating...', 'Building index...') OR a file manager/browser is open showing the generated HTML files (like index.html) in the '/home/ga/javadoc-output' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the Eclipse IDE GUI to configure and generate the Javadoc.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Eclipse 'Generate Javadoc' dialog being interacted with, rather than just a terminal window.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the Eclipse requirement by running the javadoc command directly in a terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of terminal windows where the command 'javadoc -d /home/ga/javadoc-output ...' is manually typed and executed.

integrity.description: The generated documentation appears to be genuine Javadoc HTML output.
integrity.visual_evidence: If the output folder is shown, it should contain standard Javadoc files and folders (e.g., index.html, stylesheet.css, package-list or element-list, and package directories) rather than manually created mock files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Project path: /home/ga/eclipse-workspace/DevUtils. Output path: /home/ga/javadoc-output. Expected packages to be documented: com.devutils.text, com.devutils.math, com.devutils.collection. Expected classes within those packages: StringUtils, CaseFormat, MathUtils, Statistics, ListUtils.
